title: Codex Persistent Goals and Automatic Continuation · Spiral Review
summary: Operate a long Codex task with a persistent goal, checkpoints, and user control. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Codex persistent-goal operating playbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set, view, edit, pause, resume, and clear a Codex persistent goal with the documented goal interface.
Keep long instructions in a referenced file while the goal remains concise.
Design automatic continuation around verified progress and safe terminal conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.instruction: Order the Codex Goal Command evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Codex Goal Command assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Set, view, edit, pause, resume, and clear a Codex persistent goal with the documented goal interface. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Handoff applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve goal state, decisions, tests, and next actions across sessions.
